name: The Conway National Bank
description_short: Conway National Bank is a full-service community bank headquartered in South Carolina, serving the Grand Strand area since 1903 with personal, business, and online banking.
description_long:
Conway National Bank (CNB) was established in 1903 and has maintained its position as a community-focused financial institution serving Horry County, South Carolina, and surrounding areas including the Waccamaw Neck region of Georgetown County. The bank operates 15 physical branch locations across communities including Aynor, Conway, Little River, Myrtle Beach, North Myrtle Beach, Carolina Forest, Socastee, Surfside Beach, Murrells Inlet, and Pawleys Island.

CNB offers a comprehensive range of banking services including personal banking products, business lending solutions, and digital banking access through their CNB2GO mobile and online platform. Their service portfolio includes personal loans, business loans, convenient digital banking options, and traditional deposit accounts. The bank emphasizes accessibility, including AudioEye-enabled website features and commitment to security protocols.

The bank distinguishes itself through its market dominance and industry recognition. CNB achieved #1 deposit market share position in Horry County for the fourth consecutive year (2020-2023), holding 12.48% market share as of June 30, 2023. The bank has been ranked among American Banker's "Top 200 Community Banks" and has won Forbes Best-in-State awards, demonstrating recognition beyond its local market.

As a traditional community bank, CNB's strength lies in local market knowledge and personalized service rather than technological innovation or extensive national reach. The bank's 100+ year history reflects stability and community integration, though as a regional bank it may have more limited product offerings compared to national institutions and may not offer specialized services found at larger financial institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Ranked #1 in deposit market share in Horry County for 4 consecutive years (2020-2023)
Named one of American Banker's Top 200 Community Banks
15 branch locations across South Carolina's Grand Strand area for convenient access
CNB2GO mobile and online banking available 24/7 with no fees from the bank
Over 120 years of continuous community banking since 1903
Offers both personal and business loan products with local decision-making
Strong commitment to accessibility with AudioEye-enabled website
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only serves specific South Carolina coastal markets, not available nationally
Website content does not provide specific interest rates, fees, or APR information for products
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or advanced financial products beyond basic banking
Limited information about minimum balance requirements or account opening criteria on public website
No indication of 24/7 customer service availability or multiple contact channel options
best_for: Residents of Horry County and surrounding South Carolina Grand Strand communities seeking local relationship banking
Small business owners in the Myrtle Beach, Conway, and coastal Carolina areas needing accessible business lending
Customers who value community banking stability and local decision-making over national chain convenience
diagnosis: Best for residents and business owners in South Carolina's Grand Strand (Horry County area) who prioritize personalized community banking and local market expertise. Main caveat: This is a strictly regional bank with no national presence or online-only option, and limited product information is publicly available—potential customers should visit a branch or call for specific rates, fees, and detailed product terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Conway National Bank in South Carolina serves the Grand Strand area with personal, business, and online banking since 1903.
